title: Three-Pane Ratatui Workspace Integration
summary: Deliver the v0.1 TUI workspace: left navigation, main lens, right inspector, bottom command bar, reducer-driven state, local object jumps, Function Radar, analysis memory, findings, export feedback, and terminal-size fallbacks.

files.purpose: Ratatui app state, reducer, event loop, layout, lenses, inspector, command bar, render tests.
open/tui command wiring and non-interactive error handling.
Shared view models consumed by TUI without direct DB access during rendering.
Reducer tests, TestBackend snapshots, viewport fallback tests, command integration smoke tests.
Minimal v0.1 usage notes if documentation is introduced during implementation.

actions: Implement an Elm-style TUI loop: Event -> Action -> reducer update -> view model load -> render; avoid direct SQLite queries inside render functions.
Build a stable three-pane layout with left Workspace navigation, center Main View, right Inspector, and bottom command/status bar.
Limit v0.1 workspace entries to Overview, Binary Map, Function Radar, Functions, Strings, Imports, Notes, and Findings; do not show Trace/Diff/Firmware/Crash/Protocol/Memory Labs as shipped entries.
Render Function Radar with score, function/name/address, size/span, calls/imports, strings, structured reasons, boundary_confidence, tags/status, and deterministic selection.
Render Inspector from ObjectRef with metadata, score reasons, related strings/imports/xrefs, tags, notes, linked findings, backlinks, and broken-link warnings.
Wire command mode to the shared command parser/resolver/executor for find, xrefs, tag, note, rename, status, finding, export, back, and forward.
Add state reducers for lens switching, selection movement, navigate_to, history back/forward, command errors, export success/warnings, and project open/index status.
Add terminal-size fallbacks so small terminals keep command/status visible and do not overlap left, main, inspector, or command regions.

criteria: `rg "ratatui|crossterm" crates/revdeck-tui Cargo.toml` confirms the TUI stack.
`rg "Workspace|Main View|Inspector|Command" crates/revdeck-tui` confirms the three-pane plus command/status structure.
`rg "Overview|Binary Map|Function Radar|Strings|Imports|Notes|Findings" crates/revdeck-tui` confirms v0.1 lenses and excludes future Labs from primary nav.
`rg "TestBackend|reducer" crates/revdeck-tui tests/tui` confirms deterministic TUI state/render coverage.
A fixture project can be opened in TUI, navigate Function Radar -> reason evidence -> note/tag -> finding -> export status without direct render-time DB mutation.

risks: Render-driven DB access will make TUI tests brittle and hide state bugs.
Showing roadmap Labs in v0.1 nav can create false expectations and scope creep.
Command integration must use the shared resolver; otherwise TUI shortcuts and command bar may mutate different state paths.
